description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone, featuring a rectangular base unit with a rotary dial on its front face. The handset rests atop the base, connected by a coiled black cord, and the receiver cradle is clearly visible. The phone is a muted gray color, with a transparent rotary dial and a few colored buttons or indicators on the base. The object sits on a wooden surface, which is not part of the phone itself. The phone's design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century models, commonly found in homes and offices before the advent of digital keypads. The materials appear to be primarily plastic with some metal components inside for the rotary mechanism and wiring.
description: A vintage gray corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and a visible receiver cradle, resting on a wooden surface. The phone has a classic, sturdy design typical of mid-century home or office use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, colored buttons, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base and the profile of the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the handset and the cord trailing away from the base.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying both the rotary dial and the coiled cord, with the handset clearly visible.
